message:
feat(hero): Enable Three.js scene on all devices with adaptive quality

Removes the desktop-only restriction for the Three.js WebGL scene,
allowing it to render on mobile devices with adaptive quality settings:

Changes to Hero Component:
- Removes media query check that previously disabled Three.js on
  screens smaller than 1024px (lg breakpoint)
- Unifies the scene container: removes separate mobile/desktop wrappers
- Updates pointer-events handling: disabled on mobile, enabled on desktop
- Simplifies effect cleanup since media query listeners are removed
- Updates comment to reflect new "adaptive quality + gradients" approach

Removes use-reduced-motion.ts Hook:
- This standalone hook is no longer needed as motion preferences are
  now handled within the consolidated use-mobile-optimizations.ts hook
- The hook's functionality (detecting prefers-reduced-motion) was
  redundant with the quality detection system

The result is a unified rendering path where all devices get the full
Three.js experience, with the quality system automatically degrading
visuals appropriately for device capabilities.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
